title_en: 4. Craving after their guidance to the right path even if they
title_ar: المسألة الرابعة: الحرص على هدايتهم إلى الصراط المستقيم
content_en:
be non-Muslims. (\o :.:.> L.;...l•.J.r-) ~But if they endeavour to make you associate with Me, that of which you have no knowledge; then obey them not; but behave kindly with them in the world, and follow the path of him who turns to Me in repentance and obedience. Then to Me will be 58 Sal/i~ al-Jami', hadith no. 3030. your return, and I shall tell you what you used to do.. (~r'an 31: 15) 2. Allah(~) says about Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) (9.): j~ il C{t) tA ll:~ 5'6 .t; ~Y.t ~ ~ ~f,~ If::. . ~> ~r > ·> .:j' >" .,, ~ G~ · ~1 ~~ ~}:i ;i;.~ ~ - !:J ~ . f.

J ~ • r:- , . - ,-;.., ~:~ ~t~ c ~ff u ~:, ~ Jl ?.~ ® , , .-;: .. ,, . , ,, -: .. ,,...,,. / t...:- ~ .r ,,, .I' ,, ..... •f 5-,Lz:':I\ ~ . ~ I ~~ ":J ?~ ~ ~r U..~ t.4--, ,, » ,, ,, " ,, ,,,, 1 > "t • ' t..:' f!A . .r ,, "'.:11 ' "v" ~ ~\~ ~ L)I ~\.:I -gl '-?.~ ~ \.; "'" ir°~ <)ti , , ...(~ (:j ' . ,1 ·111 :' ~~ ./;:11 (fo-t, :~_,·•;.,.-) " ' { ~ -~~ u ~ ~ J ' ~And mention in the Book [the story of] Ibrahim [Abraham]. Verily, he was a man of truth, a prophet. When he said to his father: 'O' my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and can not avail you in anything? O' my father!

Verily, there has come tome of the knowledge, that which came not to you. So folfow me, I will guide you to the Straight Path. O' my father! Worship not the Shay(iin [Satan]. Verily, Shaytiin has ever been a rebel against the Most Beneficent [Allah].

O' my father! Verily I fear lest a torment from the Most Beneficent [Allah] should overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shay[iin [in Hellfire]) (Qur 'an 19: 41-45) 3. Allah ((~) says: ~O', you who believe! Stand out firmly for justice, as witnesses to Allah, even though it be against yourselves, or your parents, .or your kin, ... ~ (Qur 'an 4: 135) The above-stated verses indicate that there should be a place of honour in the son's heart towards his parents; that the first he advises, guides to the right path and directs, are those who are nearest [of kin] to him: his parents then his kith and kin, for guiding parents and directing them to Allah's path is of dutifulness to them. 4. Asma' hint Abi Bakr(~) 'may Allah be pleased with her', said: "My mother came to me during the life time of Allah's Messenger(~) and she was a mushrikah (polytheist) yet.

I said to Allah's Messenger - seeking his verdict- 'My mother has come to me and she desires to receive a reward from me, shall I grant her what she wanted and be kind towards her?'" The Prophet (~) said: "Yes, keep good relation with her. " 59 59 Al-Bukhari, See $alfi~ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 5. In another hadith Asma' hint Abi Bakr (~ ) said: "My mother came to me recently and she desired to receive a reward from me, and she was a mushrikah and she severely detested Islam." I said: "O' Messenger of Allah, my mother came to me and she detests Islam severely, she is a mushrikah, shall I keep good relations with her?" The Prophet (~) said: "Yes. Keep good relation with her. " 60 6.

Abu Hurayrah (~) said: "I always used to call my mother to embrace Islam when she was a mushrikah. One day, I tried to call her to embrace Islam, but she (instead) abused Allah's Messenger. Thereafter, I came to the Messenger of Allah (~) with my eyes shedding tears." I said: "O' Allah's Messenger, I used to call my mother to Islam and she used to refuse. Today, I called her (to Islam) and she talked obscenely about you, so, please pray to Allah to guide her to Islam. The Prophet said: "O' Allah, guide Abu Hurayrah's mother (to Islam).

Hearing that, I went out seeing good omen in the Prophet's invocation. When I reached the door (of my house), I found it closed. My mother heard the creaking of my feet, 60 Abu Dawiid, See Sa~iJJ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. tlh.eireupon she said: 'Stay where you are, Abu Hura'Yrah·'· r eouid. hear the jogging of water (she was: taking. a. bath): He (Abu Hurayrah} said 'She bathed, tften,wore her mantle, but was so much in a hurcytowear her head veil, and quickly opened the door and said: 'O' Abu Hurayrah~ I bear witness that there irr no, god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His Slave and Messenger.'' " Abu Hurayrah said: "I returned joyfully to Allah's Messenger(~) and my e:yes shedding tears and said: 'O' Mes-senger of Allah, hear the glad tidings, Allah has just granted: you your invocation, and guided Abu Hurayrah's mother (to Islam).'" The Prophet (~) praised Allah and said nice words (on this occasion). I (Abu Hurayrah) then said: "O' Messenger of Allah, invoke Allah that believing people love my mother, and me and that we love them. The Prophet (~) then said: 'O' Allah let the believing slaves (Muslims) love this:bondman (Abu Hurayrah) and his mother, and make them love the believing people.

Thereafter, no Muslim ever hears about me or sees me but likes me.' " 61 7. Mu~'ab ibn Sa'd (~) narrated from his father (Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqa~) saying that some verses of the Holy Quran 61 Muslim, vol. 7 hadith no. 165. concerning him were revealed. He (Sa'd) said: "Sa"d's mother swore that she would never talk to him till he disbelieves in his religion; and she would never eat ec drink." She said: "You claim that Allah has enjoined on you to be dutiful to your parents, and 1 am your mot.her, and I command you to do this (i.e., disbelieve in Islam). She remained in that state (i.e., not eating nor drinking) for three· days, till she fll!nted out of exhaustion: A son of hers named 'Umarah provided her with water (then she drank), then she invoked for Allah's curse against Sa'd. Thereupon, Allah (~) revealed the following verses: ~A -. ,{ ~> .,,,,. .,. /.,,, •., ,.,.,,.,,,. ,,.,,:l.... ~And We have enjoined upon man goodness to parents .. ) (Qur 'an 29: 8) r4 ~~ ~ ;:J ~ ~ ~~ j ~ ~\ ~ 0~J ... , ~® ... t..,~ c;~, ~ 4;~~ ,~;=¥, ~ (\o :.:,L:......J .•.J_,....) •But if they encleavour to make you associate with Me, that of which you have no knowledge, then obey them not, but behave kindly with them in the world.

Jt. (Qur 'an 31: 15) 62 62 Muslim, voL 7, hadith nos. 125 & 126. The Parents' favour to their children is great Abu Hurayrah C~) narrated that the Prophet (~) said: "A son (or daughter) would never recompense his/ her) parent's favour except when he or she (the son or the daughter) finds him/her (the parent) a slave, buys him/her, then manumits him/her). " 63 Abusing and cursing one's parents is a great sin I. 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-' A~(~) related: The Prophet (~ ) said: "It is of the greatest sins that a man curses his parents.

It was asked: 'O' Allah's Messenger! How does a man curse his (own) parents?' He said: 'The man abuses the father of another man, and then the latter abuses the father of the former, and abuses his mother, and then the latter abuses the former's mother.' " 64 2. In another hadith it is reported: 63 Muslim, vol. 4, hadith no. 218. Sa/fib al-Jami', hadith no. 7498. 64 Al-Bukhari, Muslim, and Abu Dawud. See Sabib at-Targhih wat-Tarhib.

"It is of the greatest sins that a man curses his parents." It was asked: "O' Allah's Messenger! How does a man curse his parents?'' He (~) said: "A man curses another man's father, and the latter curses his father or he curses another man's mother, and the latter curses his mother. " 65 3. 'Amir ibn Wiithilah (~) narrated: "I had been with 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (~) when a man came to him and said: 'What had the Prophet (~) told you secretly (before he died)?'" He ('Ali) got angry and said: "The Prophet had never told me anything secretly, to the exclusion of other people, but he had only told me four words (things)." The man said: "What are they, O' Commander of the believers?" He said that the Prophet (~) said: "May Allah curse him, he who curses his parents. May Allah curse him, the one who slaughters (as a sacrifice) for other than Allah.

May Allah curse him, who protects and supports an innovator in religion. And may Allah curse him, who changes the marks (that show the boundaries) of lands (in order to usurp the properties of other people). " 66 [These acts were done by the people of the Days of ignorance (pre-Islamic era), who were polytheists.] 65 Bukhari and Muslim. See Sa~1~ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 66 Muslim, vol. 6, hadith no. 84. 4. lbn 'Abbas (~) related that the Prophet (ffi) said: "Accursed is he who abuses his father. Accursed is he who abuses his mother.

Accursed is he who slaughters (as a sacrifice) for other than Allah. Accursed is he who changes the marks (or boundaries) of lands (to usurp others' lands unjustly). Accursed is he who misleads a blind person. Accursed is he who has sexual intercourse with a beast, and cursed is he who follows the act of the people of Lut (Lot)(~) (i.e., sodomy). " 67 The son is of his father's earnings 1.

Jabir ibn 'Abdullah(~) narrated that a man came to the Prophet (~ ) and said: "I have money and children, and my father wants to take my money by force. The Prophet said: 'You and your money are for (or under service of) your father.'" 2. 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'A~ (~) reported: "Once a nomad came to the Prophet and said: 'I own money and I have children, and my father wants to take my money (i.e., take it all).'" He (ffi) 67 Sa/Ji/J al-Jami', hadith no. 5767. said: "You and your money are for your father (i.e., under his service). Your children are the best of your earnings, so eat and benefit from your children's earnings. " 68 3.

'A'ishah, (~ ) related that the Prophet (~ ) said: "The best of that from which you could eat is that which you earned yourselves, and your sons are of your earnings. " 69 4. 'A'ishah, (~), narrated in another hadith: The best of that from which man could eat and benefit is from his earnings, and his son is (considered) of his earnings. 70 5. Yet in another hadith she (~ ) said [from the Prophet . (~)]: "The man's son is of his earnings, the best of his earnings, so eat of their money or properties (i.e., get benefit from them)." 71 6.

'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (~ ) reported: "A man came to the Prophet and said: 'O' Allah's 68 lrwq. ' al-Ghalil, hadith no. 838; Sa~i~ al-Jami ', hadith no. 1499. 69 Ibid, hadith no. 1626; Sa~i~ al-Jami', hadith no. 1562. 70 Ibid, hadith no. 838. 71 !fa~i~ al-Jami', hadith no. 6996. Messenger! My father took my money.' " The Prophet (~) gave him a credit, "You and your money are properties of your father." 72 7. In another report, Ibn 'Umar (~) related that the Prophet (~ ) said: "The son (off-spring} is of the father's earnings.

" 73 8. 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (~ ) narrated: "A man came to the Prophet, GOmplaining against his father saying: 'He took my money."' The Prophet said: "Have you been informed that you and your money are of your father's earnings?" 74 Dutifulness to parents after their death 1. 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (~) had a donkey. When he would leave for Makkah, he would change his ride to the donkey when he was tired of riding the camel. He had a turban which he used to wear on his head.

One day, while he was riding his donkey, a nomad came across him. 'Abdullah said: "Are you not the son of so and so?" The nomad said: 72 /rw< J · al-Ghan!, hadith no. 838. 73 Ibid, hadith no. 838; $abib al-Jami·, hadith no. 7039. 74 Silsilat al-Abadith ~-Sabibah, hadith no. 1548. "Yes, I am." 'Abdullah thereafter gave him the donkey and said, "Ride this," and gave him the turban and said, "Wrap your head with it." Some of his companions said to him: "May Allah forgive you, you gave this nomad the donkey which you used to ride when you felt tired of riding the camel, and the turban with which you used to wrap Y':mr head?" 'Abdullah said: "I heard Allah's Messenger(~) say: 'It is one of the most righteous deeds that a man should keep good relations with his father's friends and beloved ones after his death. The nomad's father was a friend for 'Umar (i.e., my father).' " 75 2. Abu Bardah stated: "I came to Madinah, then 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar came to me and said: 'Do you know why I have come to you.' Abu Bardah replied: 'No.' Then, he said: 'Whoever wants to keep good relations with his father, though he is in the grave, should keep good relation with his father's friends after his death.

And my father ('Umar) and your father used to be good friends, and I wanted to keep that relation.' " 76 3. 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar narrated that the Prophet(~) said: "It is of righteousness to keep good relation with your father's friends. " 77 75 Muslim, vol. 8, hadith no. 6. 76 Sa!Ji/J at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 77 Sa/Ji/J al-Jami ', hadith no. 5777. Parents should raise their children well to make them righteous Abu Hurayrah (~) narrated that the Prophet (*) said: "When a man dies, his acts come to an end, with the exception of three: 'A recurring charity, knowledge from which people get benefited, and a pious son78 who prays for him.' " 79 2. Abu Qatadah (~) related that the Messenger of Allah (*) said: "The best three things a man could leave after his death are: 'A pious son who would pray for him (asks for Allah's forgiveness for him), a recurring charity the reward of which would go to him (i.e. the deceased) and knowledge by which people get benefited.' " 80 3.

Abu Hurayrah (~) reported that the Messenger of Allah (~) said: "Verily, the acts of which rewards follow the believer after his death are: 'Knowledge, which he 78 The Prophet said: 'Pious because rewards would not be gained through an evil son.' 79 Muslim, vol. 5, hadith no. 73, A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. 80 Ibn Miijah and others, refer to A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. taught and spread (among people), a pious son he left behind (alive to pray for him), a Holy Quran which he left as inheritance, a mosque which he built, a house for the wayfarer which he built, a canal he dug for water to flow through or a charity which he gave out of his wealth while healthy and alive, all these (by reward) follow him after his death.' " 81 4. lbn 'Abbas (•) reported that Sa'd ibn Ubadah (~) came to ask for Allah's Messenger's verdict. He (Sa'd) said: "My mother died before fulfilling a vow she made in her life, (what should I do?)." He (~ ) said: "You should fulfill it on her behalf. " 82 5. lbn 'Abbas (•) stated: 'A woman went on a journey by sea, and she made a vow that if Allah (~) saved her, she would observe fasting for a whole month. However, she did not fast till she died. A relative woman of hers (may be her sister or daughter) came to the Prophet (~ ) and told him the whole story.

He (~ ) s~id: "What do you say if she was indebted, would you pay back her debt?" The woman said: "Yes." He said: "Allah's debts have more right to be repaid; 81 lbn Majah and others, A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. 82 Bukhari and Muslim and others. Refer to A~kam al-l'and a 'iz, p. 170. Observe $awm (fasting) on your mother's behalf." 83 6. 'A'ishah, (~) narrated: "A man came to the Prophet (~ ) and said: 'My mother died suddenly and she had not had the power to write her will, and I think that if she had the power to talk, she would have given charities (of her money). Would there be any reward for her and me if I give out charities on her behalf?' ;, He replied: "Yes.

Give out charities on her behalf. " 84 7. lbn 'Abbas (~) reported: Sa'd ibn 'Ubadah's mother died while he was travelling away. He (Sa'd) said: "O' Allah's Messenger! My mother died while I was away, would it benefit her if I give out charities on her behalf?" He(~ ) said: "Yes." He (Sa'd) then said: - , I "Then bear witness that I have given the fruitful orchard (which she left behind) as a charity on her behalf. " 85 8.

Abu Hurayrah (~) related: "A man came to the Prophet and said: 'My father 83 Abu Dawiid and others. See A~kiim al-Janq 'iz, p. 169. 84 Bukhari and Muslim and others, See Abkam al-Janq 'iz, p. 172. 85 Bukhari and others. A~kiim al-Janq 'iz, p. 172. died and left some money behind, and he had not written a will, would it expiate that I give charities out on his behalf?' The Prophet said: 'Yes.' " 86 9. Buraydah (~) narrated: "Once, I was sitting with the Prophet (~) when a woman came and said: 'I have given my mother a slave-girl of mine, as a charity, then she (my mother) died. What about the slave girl?' He (~ ) said: 'You shall take your reward (for your charity) and you shall get her (the slave girl) back, because of 'the inheritance (law).' She said: 'O' Allah's Messenger, she died and she ought to have observed $awm (fasting) for one month, shall I observe Sawm on her behalf?' He said: 'Yes, fast on her behalf.' She said: 'She never performed I:Iajj, can I perform I:Iajj on her behalf?' He said: 'Yes.

Perform I:Iajj on her behalf.' " 87 10. Abu Hurayrah (~) reported that the Prophet(~) said: "A person's rank would be elevated in Paradise, and he would say: 'Why was I offered this honour?' It would be said (to him): 'This is due to your son's invocations for forgiveness for you.' " 88 86 Muslim and others. Abkam al-Jan~ "iz, p. l 72. 87 Muslim, vol. 3, hadith no. 156. 88 $abib al-Jami', hadith no. l 613 . Finally, dear Muslim brother and sister, you now know many of the Qur'anic verses that speak of the obligations of being dutiful to one's parents, and treating them kindly, and the verses that describe the Prophets' kind11ess towards their parents and how they invoked Allah ( ~) for their good. And you know now, through the Qur'anic verses and the hadiths, that dutifulness to parents is always joined with worshipping Allah(~).

Dutifulness to parents admits one to Paradise, for Allah's pleasure comes from parents' pleasure, and Allah's anger comes from parents' anger. Besides, undutifulness to parents is forbidden as stated in the Quran and the Prophet's Sunnah. Undutifulness to parents is also considered one of the greatest sins against which Allah's Messenger (~ ) warned us. Being kind to them admits us to Paradise by Allah's Will, for Paradise lies under their feet. We also knew that Parents ' invocations are certainly answered, and the parents' favour is very great on us.

The Prophet (~), forbade us from cursing or abusing our parents, and this is done by abstaining from abusing or cursing others' parents. The Prophet (*) affirmed that we are of our parents' earnings, and that we, ourselves, belong to them, as well as our properties. The Prophet (*) also commanded us to be dutiful to our parents after their death. and that is done by keeping good relation with their beloved ones. From all these, dear Muslim brother and sister, we learned from the Qur'anic verses and the Prophetic hadiths, that dutifulness to paren°ts is of paramount importance. Though even one verse commanding us to be dutiful to our parents, would suffice us and urge us to do so.

How is the case then, when there are many verses and Prophetic hadiths concerning this matter? You should, dear Muslim brother and sister, be dutiful to your parents and behave towards them as Allah (~) and the Prophet(~), enjoined on you; i.e., to behave well, giving them their rights, so that you could gain happiness in this world and in the Hereafter. Allah ( ~) says: (Y•\ :o~lo;_,.....) ~And there are mel). who say, 'Our Lord! Give us in this world that which is good and in the Hereafter that which is good and save us from the Torment of Fire) (Qur 'an 2: 201) The last of our invocations is praise be to Allah(~ ), the Lord of the world (mankind, jinn, and all that exists). title_id: 4. Merindukan Petunjuk Mereka ke Jalan yang Benar
content_id:
Meskipun orang tua bukan Muslim. Allah berfirman: "Dan jika keduanya memaksamu untuk mempersekutukan Aku dengan sesuatu yang engkau tidak mempunyai ilmu tentang itu, maka janganlah engkau menaati keduanya, dan pergaulilah keduanya di dunia dengan baik, dan ikutilah jalan orang yang kembali kepada-Ku." (Al-Quran 31:15)

Allah berfirman tentang Nabi Ibrahim (Abraham): "Dan ceritakanlah (kisah) Ibrahim di dalam Kitab (Al-Quran) ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan lagi seorang Nabi. Ingatlah ketika ia berkata kepada bapaknya: 'Wahai bapakku, mengapa kamu menyembah sesuatu yang tidak mendengar, tidak melihat dan tidak dapat menolong kamu sedikitpun? Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya telah datang kepadaku sebahagian ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak datang kepadamu, maka ikutilah aku, niscaya aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu jalan yang lurus. Wahai bapakku, janganlah kamu menyembah syaitan. Sesungguhnya syaitan itu durhaka kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah. Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan ditimpa azab dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu menjadi kawan bagi syaitan.'" (Al-Quran 19:41-45)

Allah berfirman: "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, jadilah kamu orang yang benar-benar menegakkan keadilan, menjadi saksi karena Allah walaupun terhadap dirimu sendiri atau ibu bapak dan kaum kerabatmu..." (Al-Quran 4:135)

Ayat-ayat yang telah disebutkan menunjukkan bahwa harus ada tempat kehormatan dalam hati seorang anak terhadap orang tuanya; bahwa orang pertama yang ia nasihatkan, dibimbing ke jalan yang benar, dan diarahkan adalah mereka yang paling dekat dengannya: orang tuanya kemudian kerabat-kerabatnya, karena membimbing orang tua dan mengarahkan mereka ke jalan Allah adalah bagian dari berbakti kepada mereka.

Asma' binti Abu Bakar ra berkata: "Ibuku datang kepadaku pada masa hidup Rasulullah ﷺ dan ia adalah seorang musyrik. Aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ — meminta fatwa beliau — 'Ibuku datang kepadaku dan ia ingin mendapat kebaikan dariku, haruskah aku memberinya apa yang ia inginkan dan bersikap baik kepadanya?'" Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Ya, jaga hubungan baik dengannya." (Al-Bukhari)

Dalam hadis lain, Asma' binti Abu Bakar ra berkata: "Ibuku datang kepadaku baru-baru ini dan ia ingin mendapat kebaikan dariku, dan ia adalah seorang musyrik dan sangat membenci Islam." Aku berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, ibuku datang kepadaku dan ia sangat membenci Islam, ia adalah seorang musyrik, haruskah aku menjaga hubungan baik dengannya?" Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Ya. Jaga hubungan baik dengannya."

Berbakti kepada orang tua juga mencakup mendoakan mereka agar mendapat hidayah menuju kebenaran, meskipun mereka bukan Muslim. Seorang anak yang beriman harus terus mendoakan orang tuanya yang non-Muslim agar mendapat petunjuk Allah, sambil tetap bersikap baik dan penuh hormat kepada mereka dalam kehidupan sehari-hari, sebagaimana yang dicontohkan oleh para Nabi dan Sahabat.
content_ar: be non-المسلمون. (\o :.:.> L.;...l•.J.r-) ~But إذا they endeavour to make you associate with Me, that of which you have no العلم; then obey them not; but behave kindly with them in the الدنيا, and follow the الطريق of him who turns to Me in التوبة and obedience. Then to Me will be 58 Sal/i~ al-Jami', الحديث no. 3030. your return, and I shall tell you what you used to do.. (~r'an 31: 15) 2. الله(~) says about النبي إبراهيم ﷺ (إبراهيم) (9.): j~ il C{t) tA ll:~ 5'6 .t; ~Y.t ~ ~ ~f,~ إذا::. . ~> ~r > ·> .:j' >" .,, ~ G~ · ~1 ~~ ~}:i ;i;.~ ~ - !:J ~ . f. J ~ • r:- , . - ,-;.., ~:~ ~t~ c ~ff u ~:, ~ Jl ?.~ ® , , .-;: .. ,, . , ,, -: .. ,,...,,. / t...:- ~ .r ,,, .I' ,, ..... •f 5-,Lz:':I\ ~ . ~ I ~~ ":J ?~ ~ ~r U..~ t.4--, ,, » ,, ,, " ,, ,,,, 1 > "t • ' t..:' f!A . .r ,, "'.:11 ' "v" ~ ~\~ ~ L)I ~\.:I -gl '-?.~ ~ \.; "'" ir°~ <)ti , , ...(~ (:j ' . ,1 ·111 :' ~~ ./;:11 (fo-t, :~_,·•;.,.-) " ' { ~ -~~ u ~ ~ J ' ~And mention in the الكتاب [the story of] إبراهيم ﷺ [إبراهيم]. Verily, he was a man of الحق, a النبي. عندما قال to his الأب: 'O' my الأب! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and can not avail you in anything? O' my الأب! Verily, there has come tome of the العلم, that which came not to you. لذا folfow me, I will guide you to the Straight الطريق. O' my الأب! Worship not the Shay(iin [الشيطان]. Verily, Shaytiin has ever been a rebel against the Most Beneficent [الله]. O' my الأب! Verily I الخوف lest a torment from the Most Beneficent [الله] should overtake you, لذا that you become a companion of Shay[iin [in النار]) (Qur 'an 19: 41-45) 3. الله ((~) says: ~O', you who believe! Stand out firmly for العدل, as witnesses to الله, حتى وإن it be against yourselves, or your الوالدان, .or your kin, ... ~ (Qur 'an 4: 135) The above-stated الآيات indicate that there should be a place of honour in the son's القلب towards his الوالدان; that the أولاً he advises, guides to the الحق الطريق and directs, are those who are nearest [of kin] to him: his الوالدان then his kith and kin, for guiding الوالدان and directing them to الله's الطريق is of dutifulness to them. 4. Asma' hint Abi Bakr(~) 'may الله be pleased with her', said: "My الأم came to me خلال the الحياة time of الله's Messenger(~) and she was a mushrikah (polytheist) yet. I said to الله's Messenger - seeking his verdict- 'My الأم has come to me and she desires to receive a الأجر from me, shall I grant her what she wanted and be kind towards her?'" النبي ﷺ (~) said: "Yes, keep الخير relation with her. " 59 59 البخاري, See $alfi~ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 5. In another الحديث Asma' hint Abi Bakr (~ ) said: "My الأم came to me recently and she desired to receive a الأجر from me, and she was a mushrikah and she severely detested الإسلام." I said: "O' Messenger of الله, my الأم came to me and she detests الإسلام severely, she is a mushrikah, shall I keep الخير relations with her?" النبي ﷺ (~) said: "Yes. Keep الخير relation with her. " 60 6. أبو هريرة (~) said: "I always used to call my الأم to embrace الإسلام عندما she was a mushrikah. يوماً ما, I tried to call her to embrace الإسلام, but she (instead) abused الله's Messenger. Thereafter, I came to الرسول ﷺ of الله (~) with my eyes shedding tears." I said: "O' الله's Messenger, I used to call my الأم to الإسلام and she used to refuse. Today, I called her (to الإسلام) and she talked obscenely about you, لذا, please pray to الله to guide her to الإسلام. النبي ﷺ said: "O' الله, guide أبو هريرة's mother (to الإسلام). Hearing that, I went out seeing الخير omen in النبي ﷺ's invocation. عندما I reached the door (of my house), I found it closed. My الأم heard the creaking of my feet, 60 Abu Dawiid, See Sa~iJJ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. tlh.eireupon sقال: 'Stay where you are, Abu Hura'Yrah·'· r eouid. hear the jogging of water (she was: taking. a. bath): He (أبو هريرة} said 'She bathed, tften,wore her mantle, but was لذا much in a hurcytowear her head veil, and quickly opened the door and said: 'O' أبو هريرة~ I bear witness that there irr no, god but الله, and I bear witness that محمد ﷺ is His Slave and Messenger.'' " أبو هريرة said: "I returned joyfully to الله's Messenger(~) and my e:yes shedding tears and said: 'O' Mes-senger of الله, hear the glad tidings, الله has just granted: you your invocation, and guided أبو هريرة's الأم (to الإسلام).'" النبي ﷺ (~) praised الله and said nice words (on this occasion). I (أبو هريرة) then said: "O' Messenger of الله, invoke الله that believing people المحبة my الأم, and me and that we المحبة them. النبي ﷺ (~) then said: 'O' الله let the believing slaves (المسلمون) المحبة this:bondman (أبو هريرة) and his الأم, and make them المحبة the believing people. Thereafter, no مسلم ever hears about me or sees me but likes me.' " 61 7. Mu~'ab ibn Sa'd (~) narrated from his الأب (Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqa~) saying that some الآيات of the Holy القرآن الكريم 61 مسلم, vol. 7 الحديث no. 165. فيما يتعلق بـ him were revealed. He (Sa'd) said: "Sa"d's الأم swore that she would never talk to him till he disbelieves in his religion; and she would never eat ec drink." Sقال: "You claim that الله has enjoined on you to be dutiful to your الوالدان, and 1 am your mot.her, and I command you to do this (i.e., disbelieve in الإسلام). She remained in that state (i.e., not eating nor drinking) for three· days, till she fll!nted out of exhaustion: A son of hers named 'Umarah provided her with water (then she drank), then she invoked for الله's curse against Sa'd. Thereupon, الله (~) revealed the following الآيات: ~A -. ,{ ~> .,,,,. .,. /.,,, •., ,.,.,,.,,,. ,,.,,:l.... ~And We have enjoined upon man goodness to الوالدان .. ) (Qur 'an 29: 8) r4 ~~ ~ ;:J ~ ~ ~~ j ~ ~\ ~ 0~J ... , ~® ... t..,~ c;~, ~ 4;~~ ,~;=¥, ~ (\o :.:,L:......J .•.J_,....) •But إذا they encleavour to make you associate with Me, that of which you have no العلم, then obey them not, but behave kindly with them in the الدنيا. Jt. (Qur 'an 31: 15) 62 62 مسلم, voL 7, الحديث nos. 125 & 126. The الوالدان' النعمة to their الأولاد is great أبو هريرة C~) narrated that النبي ﷺ (~) said: "A son (or daughter) would never recompense his/ her) parent's النعمة except عندما he or she (the son or the daughter) finds him/her (the parent) a slave, buys him/her, then manumits him/her). " 63 Abusing and cursing one's الوالدان is a great الذنب I. 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-' A~(~) related: النبي ﷺ (~ ) said: "It is of the greatest الذنوب that a man curses his الوالدان. It was asked: 'O' الله's Messenger! How does a man curse his (own) الوالدان?' قال: 'The man abuses the الأب of another man, and then the latter abuses the الأب of the former, and abuses his الأم, and then the latter abuses the former's الأم.' " 64 2. In another الحديث أُفيد بأن: 63 مسلم, vol. 4, الحديث no. 218. Sa/fib al-Jami', الحديث no. 7498. 64 البخاري, مسلم, and أبو داود. See Sabib at-Targhih wat-Tarhib. "It is of the greatest الذنوب that a man curses his الوالدان." It was asked: "O' الله's Messenger! How does a man curse his الوالدان?'' He (~) said: "A man curses another man's الأب, and the latter curses his الأب or he curses another man's الأم, and the latter curses his الأم. " 65 3. 'Amir ibn Wiithilah (~) narrated: "I had been with 'علي ibn Abi Talib (~) عندما a man came to him and said: 'What had النبي ﷺ (~) told you secretly (قبل he died)?'" He ('علي) got angry and said: "النبي ﷺ had never told me anything secretly, to the exclusion of other people, but he had only told me four words (things)." The man said: "What are they, O' Commander of the المؤمنون?" قال that النبي ﷺ (~) said: "May الله curse him, he who curses his الوالدان. May الله curse him, the one who slaughters (as a sacrifice) for other than الله. May الله curse him, who protects and supports an innovator in religion. And may الله curse him, who changes the marks (that show the boundaries) of lands (من أجل usurp the properties of other people). " 66 [These acts were done by the people of the Days of ignorance (pre-Islamic era), who were polytheists.] 65 البخاري and مسلم. See Sa~1~ at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 66 مسلم, vol. 6, الحديث no. 84. 4. lbn 'Abbas (~) related that النبي ﷺ (ffi) said: "Accursed is he who abuses his الأب. Accursed is he who abuses his الأم. Accursed is he who slaughters (as a sacrifice) for other than الله. Accursed is he who changes the marks (or boundaries) of lands (to usurp others' lands unjustly). Accursed is he who misleads a blind person. Accursed is he who has sexual intercourse with a beast, and cursed is he who follows the act of the people of Lut (Lot)(~) (i.e., sodomy). " 67 The son is of his الأب's earnings 1. Jabir ibn 'Abdullah(~) narrated that a man came to النبي ﷺ (~ ) and said: "I have المال and الأولاد, and my الأب wants to take my المال by force. النبي ﷺ said: 'You and your المال are for (or under service of) your الأب.'" 2. 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'A~ (~) reported: "حين a nomad came to النبي ﷺ and said: 'I own المال and I have الأولاد, and my الأب wants to take my المال (i.e., take it all).'" He (ffi) 67 Sa/Ji/J al-Jami', الحديث no. 5767. said: "You and your المال are for your الأب (i.e., under his service). Your الأولاد are the best of your earnings, لذا eat and benefit from your الأولاد's earnings. " 68 3. 'A'ishah, (~ ) related that النبي ﷺ (~ ) said: "The best of that from which you could eat is that which you earned yourselves, and your sons are of your earnings. " 69 4. 'A'ishah, (~), narrated in another الحديث: The best of that from which man could eat and benefit is from his earnings, and his son is (considered) of his earnings. 70 5. Yet in another الحديث she (~ ) said [from النبي ﷺ . (~)]: "The man's son is of his earnings, the best of his earnings, لذا eat of their المال or properties (i.e., get benefit from them)." 71 6. 'Abdullah ابن عمر (~ ) reported: "A man came to النبي ﷺ and said: 'O' الله's 68 lrwq. ' al-Ghalil, الحديث no. 838; Sa~i~ al-Jami ', الحديث no. 1499. 69 Ibid, الحديث no. 1626; Sa~i~ al-Jami', الحديث no. 1562. 70 Ibid, الحديث no. 838. 71 !fa~i~ al-Jami', الحديث no. 6996. Messenger! My الأب took my المال.' " النبي ﷺ (~) gave him a credit, "You and your المال are properties of your الأب." 72 7. In another report, ابن عمر (~) related that النبي ﷺ (~ ) said: "The son (off-spring} is of the الأب's earnings. " 73 8. 'Abdullah ابن عمر (~ ) narrated: "A man came to النبي ﷺ, GOmplaining against his الأب saying: 'He took my المال."' النبي ﷺ said: "Have you been informed that you and your المال are of your الأب's earnings?" 74 Dutifulness to الوالدان بعد their الموت 1. 'Abdullah ابن عمر (~) had a donkey. عندما he would leave for مكة المكرمة, he would change his ride to the donkey عندما he was tired of riding the camel. He had a turban which he used to wear on his head. يوماً ما, بينما he was riding his donkey, a nomad came across him. 'Abdullah said: "Are you not the son of لذا and لذا?" The nomad said: 72 /rw< J · al-Ghan!, الحديث no. 838. 73 Ibid, الحديث no. 838; $abib al-Jami·, الحديث no. 7039. 74 Silsilat al-Abadith ~-Sabibah, الحديث no. 1548. "Yes, I am." 'Abdullah thereafter gave him the donkey and said, "Ride this," and gave him the turban and said, "Wrap your head with it." Some of his الصحابة said to him: "May الله forgive you, you gave this nomad the donkey which you used to ride عندما you felt tired of riding the camel, and the turban with which you used to wrap Y':mr head?" 'Abdullah said: "I heard الله's Messenger(~) say: 'It is one of the most الصالح deeds that a man should keep الخير relations with his الأب's friends and beloved ones بعد his الموت. The nomad's الأب was a friend for 'عمر (i.e., my الأب).' " 75 2. Abu Bardah stated: "I came to المدينة المنورة, then 'Abdullah ابن عمر came to me and said: 'Do you know why I have come to you.' Abu Bardah replied: 'No.' Then, قال: 'Whoever wants to keep الخير relations with his الأب, though he is in the القبر, should keep الخير relation with his الأب's friends بعد his الموت. And my الأب ('عمر) and your الأب used to be الخير friends, and I wanted to keep that relation.' " 76 3. 'Abdullah ابن عمر narrated that النبي ﷺ(~) said: "It is of righteousness to keep الخير relation with your الأب's friends. " 77 75 مسلم, vol. 8, الحديث no. 6. 76 Sa!Ji/J at-Targhib wat-Tarhib. 77 Sa/Ji/J al-Jami ', الحديث no. 5777. الوالدان should raise their الأولاد well to make them الصالح أبو هريرة (~) narrated that النبي ﷺ (*) said: "عندما a man dies, his acts come to an end, with the exception of three: 'A recurring الصدقة, العلم from which people get benefited, and a pious son78 who prays for him.' " 79 2. Abu Qatadah (~) related that الرسول ﷺ of الله (*) said: "The best three things a man could leave بعد his الموت are: 'A pious son who would pray for him (asks for الله's المغفرة for him), a recurring الصدقة the الأجر of which would go to him (i.e. the deceased) and العلم by which people get benefited.' " 80 3. أبو هريرة (~) reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله (~) said: "Verily, the acts of which rewards follow the المؤمن بعد his الموت are: 'العلم, which he 78 النبي ﷺ said: 'Pious لأن rewards would not be gained through an الشر son.' 79 مسلم, vol. 5, الحديث no. 73, A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. 80 Ibn Miijah and others, refer to A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. taught and spread (among people), a pious son he left behind (alive to pray for him), a Holy القرآن الكريم which he left as inheritance, a المسجد which he built, a house for the wayfarer which he built, a canal he dug for water to flow through or a الصدقة which he gave out of his الثروة بينما healthy and alive, all these (by الأجر) follow him بعد his الموت.' " 81 4. lbn 'Abbas (•) reported that Sa'd ibn Ubadah (~) came to ask for الله's Messenger's verdict. He (Sa'd) said: "My الأم died قبل fulfilling a vow she made in her الحياة, (what should I do?)." He (~ ) said: "You should fulfill it on her behalf. " 82 5. lbn 'Abbas (•) stated: 'A woman went on a journey by sea, and she made a vow that إذا الله (~) saved her, she would observe الصيام for a whole month. غير أن, she did not fast till she died. A relative woman of hers (may be her الأخت or daughter) came to النبي ﷺ (~ ) and told him the whole story. He (~ ) s~id: "What do you say إذا she was indebted, would you pay back her debt?" The woman said: "Yes." قال: "الله's debts have more الحق to be repaid; 81 lbn Majah and others, A~kam al-Jana 'iz, p. 176. 82 البخاري and مسلم and others. Refer to A~kam al-l'and a 'iz, p. 170. Observe $awm (الصيام) on your الأم's behalf." 83 6. 'A'ishah, (~) narrated: "A man came to النبي ﷺ (~ ) and said: 'My الأم died suddenly and she had not had the power to write her will, and I think that إذا she had the power to talk, she would have given charities (of her المال). Would there be any الأجر for her and me إذا I give out charities on her behalf?' ;, أجاب: "Yes. Give out charities on her behalf. " 84 7. lbn 'Abbas (~) reported: Sa'd ibn 'Ubadah's الأم died بينما he was travelling away. He (Sa'd) said: "O' الله's Messenger! My الأم died بينما I was away, would it benefit her إذا I give out charities on her behalf?" He(~ ) said: "Yes." He (Sa'd) then said: - , I "Then bear witness that I have given the fruitful orchard (which she left behind) as a الصدقة on her behalf. " 85 8. أبو هريرة (~) related: "A man came to النبي ﷺ and said: 'My الأب 83 Abu Dawiid and others. See A~kiim al-Janq 'iz, p. 169. 84 البخاري and مسلم and others, See Abkam al-Janq 'iz, p. 172. 85 البخاري and others. A~kiim al-Janq 'iz, p. 172. died and left some المال behind, and he had not written a will, would it expiate that I give charities out on his behalf?' النبي ﷺ said: 'Yes.' " 86 9. Buraydah (~) narrated: "حين, I was sitting with النبي ﷺ (~) عندما a woman came and said: 'I have given my الأم a slave-girl of mine, as a الصدقة, then she (my الأم) died. What about the slave girl?' He (~ ) said: 'You shall take your الأجر (for your الصدقة) and you shall get her (the slave girl) back, لأن of 'the inheritance (law).' Sقال: 'O' الله's Messenger, she died and she ought to have observed $awm (الصيام) for one month, shall I observe الصيام on her behalf?' قال: 'Yes, fast on her behalf.' Sقال: 'She never performed I:Iajj, can I perform I:Iajj on her behalf?' قال: 'Yes. Perform I:Iajj on her behalf.' " 87 10. أبو هريرة (~) reported that النبي ﷺ(~) said: "A person's rank would be elevated in الجنة, and he would say: 'Why was I offered this honour?' It would be said (to him): 'This is بسبب your son's invocations for المغفرة for you.' " 88 86 مسلم and others. Abkam al-Jan~ "iz, p. l 72. 87 مسلم, vol. 3, الحديث no. 156. 88 $abib al-Jami', الحديث no. l 613 . وأخيراً, dear مسلم الأخ and الأخت, you now know many of the Qur'anic الآيات that speak of the obligations of being dutiful to one's الوالدان, and treating them kindly, and the الآيات that describe the الأنبياء' kind11ess towards their الوالدان and how they invoked الله ( ~) for their الخير. And you know now, through the Qur'anic الآيات and the hadiths, that dutifulness to الوالدان is always joined with worshipping الله(~). Dutifulness to الوالدان admits one to الجنة, for الله's pleasure comes from الوالدان' pleasure, and الله's الغضب comes from الوالدان' الغضب. بالإضافة إلى, undutifulness to الوالدان is forbidden as stated in the القرآن الكريم and النبي ﷺ's السنة. Undutifulness to الوالدان is أيضاً considered one of the greatest الذنوب against which الله's Messenger (~ ) warned us. Being kind to them admits us to الجنة by الله's Will, for الجنة lies under their feet. We أيضاً knew that الوالدان ' invocations are certainly answered, and the الوالدان' النعمة is very great on us. النبي ﷺ (~), forbade us from cursing or abusing our الوالدان, and this is done by abstaining from abusing or cursing others' الوالدان. النبي ﷺ (*) affirmed that we are of our الوالدان' earnings, and that we, ourselves, belong to them, as well as our properties. النبي ﷺ (*) أيضاً commanded us to be dutiful to our الوالدان بعد their الموت. and that is done by keeping الخير relation with their beloved ones. From all these, dear مسلم الأخ and الأخت, we learned from the Qur'anic الآيات and the Prophetic hadiths, that dutifulness to paren°ts is of paramount importance. Though even one الآية commanding us to be dutiful to our الوالدان, would suffice us and urge us to do لذا. How is the case then, عندما there are many الآيات and Prophetic hadiths فيما يتعلق بـ this matter? You should, dear مسلم الأخ and الأخت, be dutiful to your الوالدان and behave towards them as الله (~) and النبي ﷺ(~), enjoined on you; i.e., to behave well, giving them their الحقوق, لذا that you could gain happiness in this الدنيا and in الآخرة. الله ( ~) says: (Y•\ :o~lo;_,.....) ~And there are mel). who say, 'Our Lord! Give us in this الدنيا that which is الخير and in الآخرة that which is الخير and save us from the Torment of Fire) (Qur 'an 2: 201) The last of our invocations is praise be to الله(~ ), the Lord of the الدنيا (mankind, الجن, and all that exists).
